name: Tofu Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Mash the tofu well. For best results, blend a firm Chinese style tofu with a smaller portion of Japanese style tofu, such as Mori-Nu Firm.
2. Line an 11-inch colander with a single layer of moistened cheesecloth.
3. Transfer the mashed tofu to the colander and press it to flatten. Fold the edges of the cheesecloth over the tofu.
4. Place a cake tin or other flat object on top of the tofu and weigh it down with a heavy object (about 5 lbs) to press out the liquid.
5. After draining for 1 hour, hollow out the tofu to within 1 inch of the colander.
6. Fill the cavity with your favorite stuffing and pack it in. Cover with the remaining tofu. Pat the surface flat and firm.
7. Oil a roast or turkey pan, place the pan over the colander, and flip so that the flat surface of the filled tofu faces down. Remove the cheesecloth.
8. Mix sesame oil and tamari to create a basting liquid. Baste the tofu with the liquid.
9. Bake covered at 400°F for 1 hour. Remove the cover, baste, and return the tofu to the oven uncovered until the top becomes golden brown (about one more hour). Baste at least once during the remaining cooking time.
From The Fund for Animals, P.O. Box 770243, Houston, TX 77215
